children.content: One US crew member has been rescued after an American fighter jet was downed over Iran, a US official has confirmed. Follow live.

items.title: World loses patience with Trump as war becomes about more than Iran

children.content: Global leaders are giving up the pretence of diplomatic niceties as they lose patience with the US president's actions.

items.title: The 20,000 sailors who have become sitting ducks in the Gulf

children.content: Interviews and first-hand accounts from sailors stranded in the Persian Gulf — under threat of drone attacks, missiles, and exploding boats — reveal an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis unfolding.

image.altText: A satellite photo of a lot of large ships floating in the sea.

items.title: Cops would not have had 'luxury' of taking Dezi Freeman alive, experts say

children.content: A former police hostage negotiator says he was sure Dezi Freeman's seven-month run from the law "was going to end in tears" and that the fugitive would never surrender.

image.altText: A black police armourned vehicle is parked next to a shipping container. A black pipe is connected between the two.

items.title: Strategically critical Pacific island divided over growing US military presence

children.content: The Pacific Island of Guam sits at the centre of a struggle between the world's most powerful military and the right of its people to determine their own future.

image.altText: Ron Acfalle paddles a canoe.

items.title: How the Iran war is pushing US ally the Philippines into economic crisis

children.content: Countries heavily reliant on imported oil are particularly vulnerable to the unfolding energy crisis caused by the US-Israel war with Iran. It's no more apparent than in South-East Asian countries like the Philippines.

items.title: The destructive path of Cyclone Narelle, pieced together frame by frame

items.title: Modern life the 'perfect tsunami' for poor liver health

items.title: How Australia's leaders have changed their language on the Iran war

items.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes

items.title: Hospitality operators call for end to Rottnest's staffing advantage

items.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff for post-election pay delay

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.
